explanation:
The natural language describes a requirement in PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode: When in expiration phase, if a spontaneous breath is not detected (meaning dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false) and it is within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] (indicated by expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag), if the user sets 'Expiratory Pause' using the expPause button (expiratoryPauseButton), then expiratoryPause must become active (i.e., Expiratory Pause should start). This is a conditional requirement that always holds when those conditions are met.

To encode this: The condition (expiratoryPhase & !dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & expiratoryPauseButton) captures the trigger. The response is expiratoryPause. The 'always' temporal operator is suitable as this requirement needs to hold throughout PSV mode operation. Durations appear as intervals in the atomic proposition and are handled by the 'expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag' signal, so additional duration in the template is not required. Template 30 (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)) best matches, where bool_exp1 is the triggering conjunction and bool_exp3 is the response (expiratoryPause).

The property describes that in PSV mode, during the expiration phase, if we are within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] (expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag), and a spontaneous breath is NOT detected (i.e., dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is FALSE), then if and when the GUI sets the Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPauseButton), the Expiratory Pause should actually start (expiratoryPause). This is captured by setting bool_exp1 as: PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & (! dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV) & expiratoryPauseButton, which encodes all the guards. The property ensures that whenever this combination of conditions holds, an expiratory pause will follow.

The natural language states a requirement in PSV Mode: If the system is in the expiratory phase, within a certain timing window ([min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag]), and no spontaneous breath is detected (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), then, if the user sets an Expiratory Pause using the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), an Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPause) must start. The corresponding atomic propositions are:
- PSVMode (for PSV Mode)
- expiratoryPhase (indicates expiration phase is active)
- dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV (spontaneous breath detection)
- expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag (timing interval)
- expiratoryPauseButton (GUI request)
- expiratoryPause (Expiratory Pause active)

The LTL property must enforce: Whenever the system is in PSV mode, in expiratory phase, in the correct time interval, and a spontaneous breath is NOT detected (so, !dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV), if the Expiratory Pause is requested, then within a short time (duration N_DURATION) the Expiratory Pause must activate.

This is of the form: G (guards -> F[0,N_DURATION] expiratoryPause), where guards = PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & (!dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV) & expiratoryPauseButton.

So, this corresponds to template #34: G (bool_exp1 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))
bool_exp1: PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & (!dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV) & expiratoryPauseButton
bool_exp3: expiratoryPause
Other bools are not used in this template.
N_DURATION is set to a reasonable value, e.g., 3 (short reaction time in clinical safety, but this can be project specific).

This LTL property states that globally (at all times), if the controller is in PSV mode, is in the expiratory phase, currently within the time interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] ('expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag'), and a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' is false), then whenever the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI ('expiratoryPauseButton'), the Expiratory Pause must start ('expiratoryPause'). This directly captures the requirement that, under these conditions, an expiratory pause initiated by the GUI must result in the expiratory pause being active. We use the template 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3))' to ensure that, whenever these preconditions hold, if the pause is requested, it will follow within an interval (N_DURATION), representing the system response allowance within the interval.

This LTL property formalizes the natural language requirement as follows: Whenever the system is in PSV mode (PSVMode is true), is in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase is true), within the time window [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] of expiration (expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag is true), and no spontaneous breath has been detected (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false), if the Expiratory Pause button is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton is true), then (F[0,N_DURATION] means 'eventually within N_DURATION steps') an Expiratory Pause must be activated (expiratoryPause becomes true) within a fixed number of time units (N_DURATION). This LTL property ensures that, under the specified conditions, setting the Expiratory Pause triggers the correct ventilatory action.

We use template 44: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)), where:
- bool_exp1 = PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & (! dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV)
- bool_exp2 = expiratoryPauseButton
- bool_exp3 = expiratoryPause
- N_DURATION = 10 (arbitrary positive integer as required)

This covers all the stated conditions and the required system response.

This LTL property describes the following behavior: Whenever the ventilator is in Pressure Support Ventilation mode (PSVMode) and it is currently in the expiration phase (expiratoryPhase), and within the time window [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] (expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag), and a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false), if the Expiratory Pause button is pressed (expiratoryPauseButton), then (almost) immediately an Expiratory Pause (expiratoryPause) must be triggered. This captures the requirement that within this specific time window, pressing the Expiratory Pause button when there's no spontaneous breath detected must result in an Expiratory Pause. The chosen template enforces that, whenever these conditions hold, in the next time step (as promptly as possible), the Expiratory Pause is initiated.

The property describes that whenever the system is in PSV mode, in expiratory phase, and during the time window between min_exp_time_psv and apnea lag ('expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag' is true), if no spontaneous breath is detected ('dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' is false), and the Expiratory Pause button is pressed ('expiratoryPauseButton' is true), then an Expiratory Pause must be initiated ('expiratoryPause' becomes true, i.e., Expiratory Pause is active). This is formalized as: globally, whenever the condition (PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & !dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV & expiratoryPauseButton) holds, it must lead to expiatoryPause holding. This captures the user requirement that Expiratory Pause shall start in the described situation (if set by GUI during expiratory phase in the specified interval, and no patient trigger is detected).

This LTL property formalizes the requirement that when in PSV Mode, during the expiration phase, and if no spontaneous breath is detected (i.e., dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false), and within the time interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] (i.e., expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag is true), if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), then an expiratory pause should start (expiratoryPause is set). This property matches the CONT.42 requirement by using the antecedent as the conjunction of all the specified conditions and the consequent as the assertion that expiratoryPause should become true. The globally operator (G) ensures this rule holds at all times, and the implication captures the conditional logic described.

This LTL property encodes the following logic: Whenever the system is in PSV Mode, in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase), within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] (expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag is true), and a spontaneous breath is NOT detected (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV is false), if the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (expiratoryPauseButton), then an Expiratory Pause must occur (expiratoryPause). This is represented using implication under a global 'always' (G), with a conjunction of conditions as the antecedent, expressing the required causality in the natural language statement. The chosen duration is 5, standing for some finite check within the interval bounds.

The natural language requirement specifies that in PSV mode, if during the expiration phase and within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] there is not a spontaneous breath (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), and the Expiratory Pause is requested by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall actually occur. 

The most appropriate template is a global response pattern conditioned on several criteria: we need to globally express that whenever all of the following hold -- controller is in PSV mode, expiration phase is active, time is within [min_exp_time_psv:apnea lag], a breath is NOT detected (¬dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV), and Expiratory Pause is set (expiratoryPauseButton) -- then eventually Expiratory Pause actually starts (expiratoryPause). 

This is a typical response/trigger pattern. Using available templates, we can encode the entire conjunction as bool_exp1 and the guarantee as bool_exp3. The template G (bool_exp1 -> F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) fits as it expresses: Globally, whenever the trigger (bool_exp1) holds, within the given interval N_DURATION, bool_exp3 must become true, which matches the requirement that the expiratory pause must start if requested under the specified conditions. N_DURATION covers the practical max expected lag between request and pause start (e.g., 2 is used here to allow nonzero duration).

Bool_exp1 captures the composite triggering condition: PSVMode & expiratoryPhase & expClock_within_interval_min_exp_time_psv_to_apnea_lag & (!dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV) & expiratoryPauseButton. Bool_exp3 is just expiratoryPause. bool_exp2 and bool_exp4 are unused.

N_DURATION is set to 2, representing a short, reasonable time window in which the pause should start after the request under given conditions.
